name: Western Xia (Tangut Empire)
note: Tangut empire in northwestern China. Controlled the Hexi Corridor and Silk Road trade. Developed a unique script modelled on Chinese characters. Resisted both the Song and Liao dynasties. Utterly destroyed by Genghis Khan's final campaign (1227) — the Mongols annihilated the Tangut state so thoroughly that it nearly vanished from historical memory.
